skill.name: variant-pathogenicity-predictor
skill.description: Combines REVEL, CADD, PolyPhen, and other in-silico scores into a weighted composite pathogenicity prediction with ACMG interpretation for genetic variant classification.
skill.summary: Integrate multiple pathogenicity scores into one composite variant classification.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 38 static findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts correspond to argparse initialization and license text, not actual cryptography. Ruby/shell backtick execution findings all point to markdown documentation code blocks showing example bash commands. The path traversal findings reference a security checklist that warns against traversal. The hardcoded URL is a source attribution link. No prompt injection attempts or semantic threats were identified. The skill is a legitimate bioinformatics tool with a simple Python script that performs arithmetic operations only, with no network calls, file I/O, or dangerous operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at scripts/main.py:51, SKILL.md:3, and SKILL.md:23. These are false positives: line 51 is argparse initialization, line 3 is the MIT license declaration, and line 23 is a markdown heading. No cryptographic operations exist anywhere in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Documentation in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 25 locations for Ruby/shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. All findings reference bash commands inside markdown fenced code blocks used for documentation and usage examples (e.g., 'python scripts/main.py --help'). These are not executable backtick commands but documentation examples showing end users how to invoke the script.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Source URL
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a GitHub URL linking to the source repository. This is a legitimate source attribution for a community-submitted skill, not an unauthorized network call or data exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Traversal in Security Checklist
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged path traversal references at SKILL.md:125 and 128. These lines are part of a security checklist that explicitly warns against path traversal ('No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'). The skill takes a defensive posture against path traversal, not implementing it.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md:56, SKILL.md:68, and audit_result_v2.json locations. These are documentation entries about output discipline and validation commands, plus a pre-computed evaluation artifact. No reconnaissance functionality exists in the skill.

content.user_title: Predict Variant Pathogenicity from Multiple Scores
content.value_statement: Bioinformatics researchers often struggle to reconcile conflicting pathogenicity predictions from different tools. This skill combines REVEL, CADD, PolyPhen, and other scores into a single weighted composite classification with ACMG guideline interpretation.
actual_capabilities: Combines REVEL, CADD, and PolyPhen scores into a weighted composite pathogenicity score
Classifies variants as Pathogenic, Likely Pathogenic, Uncertain Significance, Likely Benign, or Benign
Provides individual score breakdown alongside the composite assessment for transparency
Supports both single variant queries and VCF file batch processing
Generates structured output suitable for research documentation and review
Includes a pre-computed demo mode for quick validation of the workflow
limitations: Prediction scores must be pre-computed from external tools before using this skill
Does not perform sequence alignment, variant calling, or raw sequencing data processing
Composite score weights are fixed defaults and may not suit all research contexts
Pathogenicity prediction is a research tool and not a substitute for clinical expert review

use_cases.title: Single Variant Pathogenicity Assessment
use_cases.description: A researcher has a novel missense variant from a targeted sequencing study and needs to quickly assess its potential pathogenicity by combining multiple pre-computed scores.
use_cases.target_user: Research scientist in genetics or genomics

use_cases.title: Batch Variant Interpretation for Publications
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician has a VCF file with dozens of variants and needs consistent pathogenicity classifications with score breakdowns for supplementary materials in a manuscript.

use_cases.title: Educational Demonstration of Score Integration
use_cases.description: A student learning clinical genomics wants to understand how multiple in-silico prediction tools can be combined to produce a more robust pathogenicity call.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate student in bioinformatics or medical genetics

prompt_templates.title: Single Variant Prediction
prompt_templates.prompt: Predict pathogenicity for variant chr17:43094692:G:A in the BRCA1 gene using REVEL score 0.85, CADD score 25, and PolyPhen score 0.92.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick assessment of a single missense variant

prompt_templates.title: Batch Analysis from VCF
prompt_templates.prompt: Process the VCF file at variants.vcf and generate a pathogenicity report for all variants in the TP53 gene. Write the output to tp53_report.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple variants from a sequencing pipeline

prompt_templates.title: Custom Score Weights
prompt_templates.prompt: Predict pathogenicity for variant chr7:55249071:G>A with REVEL 0.91, CADD 30, SIFT 0.01, and MutationTaster 0.99. Use equal weights for all four scores instead of the default weighting.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher wants to customize the integration method

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Analysis Across Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the pathogenicity predictor in demo mode and then compare the composite score method against the individual REVEL, CADD, and PolyPhen classifications. Explain where they agree and disagree.
prompt_templates.scenario: Validation and method comparison for a methods section

output_examples.input: Predict pathogenicity for BRCA1 variant chr17:43094692:G>A with REVEL 0.85, CADD 25, and PolyPhen 0.92.
output: All three individual scores are in the pathogenic range. The composite score of 0.813 strongly supports a Likely Pathogenic classification under ACMG guidelines.

output_examples.input: Run the demo mode and describe the results.
output: The demo confirms the prediction pipeline is working correctly. All three scores contribute to a consistent Likely Pathogenic call with high confidence.

best_practices: Validate the input variant format (chr:pos:ref:alt) before running predictions to avoid silent failures
Document the version and source of each prediction score for reproducibility in research records
Cross-reference composite results with population frequency data and family segregation evidence
anti_patterns: Using this skill without pre-computed REVEL, CADD, or PolyPhen scores, as the skill does not compute these values
Relying solely on the composite score for clinical decision-making without expert review
Applying the default composite score weights without considering domain or gene-specific calibration

faq.question: What prediction scores does this skill integrate?
faq.answer: The skill integrates REVEL, CADD, and PolyPhen scores by default. It also supports SIFT and MutationTaster when provided as additional inputs.

faq.question: How is the composite pathogenicity score calculated?
faq.answer: The composite score is a weighted average: REVEL contributes 40 percent, CADD contributes 30 percent, and PolyPhen contributes 30 percent. CADD scores are normalized to a 0 to 1 range by dividing by 30.

faq.question: Is this tool validated for clinical use?
faq.answer: No. This skill is a research tool designed for exploratory data analysis. ACMG interpretations are guideline summaries and are not a substitute for clinical expert review.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces a structured text output with the composite score, pathogenicity classification, and individual score breakdown. VCF batch processing outputs JSON format.

faq.question: Can I add custom prediction scores to the analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide additional scores such as SIFT or MutationTaster values in your request. The skill can incorporate custom scores with adjusted weights.

faq.question: How should I interpret Uncertain Significance results?
faq.answer: Variants classified as Uncertain Significance (composite score between 0.3 and 0.7) indicate conflicting or intermediate evidence. Additional data such as population frequency and segregation analysis is recommended.
